inflection_resolutions.rationale: Title-only is the canonical minimal kanban card model, has well-known training-data precedent, identical implementation complexity vs title+description (both store JSON objects), introduces no new dependencies, and is trivially reversible by extending the card schema with an optional description field later. Honors the simplest-within-reason principle from the ledger and matches A3 (title minimum). Storing cards as objects (not bare strings) keeps the schema forward-compatible per A3's what_breaks_if_wrong note.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Cards are stored as objects with at least an id and a title field; description and other metadata fields are absent in v1.
createCard accepts only a title (string) and a target listId; it does not accept a description parameter.
The rendered card DOM element shows the title text and does not render a description element, modal trigger, or expand affordance.

inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Lists are fixed in their creation order; only cards are draggable
inflection_resolutions.rationale: The prompt names DnD in the context of cards. Card-only DnD is the canonical kanban interaction in training data, has lower implementation complexity (one drag type, one drop-target type), introduces no new dependencies, and is trivially reversible by adding a list-level drag handle later. Avoids drop-zone conflict cases between card-drag and list-drag. Matches IP2 default.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: List header DOM elements do not have draggable=true and do not register dragstart handlers.

absent_attributes: draggable=true on list root or list header

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: The state-model exposes no reorderList mutation; list order is determined by creation order only.

sections.name: state-model
sections.charter: Define the pure-JavaScript data model for the kanban board (board, lists, cards) and provide reducer-style mutation functions that return new state without touching the DOM. Includes id generation, validation, and a subscribe/notify mechanism so other sections can react to state changes. This is the central section: persistence, ui-render, dnd, and interactions all depend on its API. Cards are objects with id and title only (per IP1); lists are ordered by creation and not reorderable (per IP2).
sections.acceptance: Exposes createList, renameList, deleteList, createCard, editCard, deleteCard, moveCard, reorderCard mutations; getState returns current board; subscribe registers change listeners; mutations validate inputs and produce predictable state transitions; no DOM access anywhere in this module.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Exposes createList, renameList, deleteList, createCard, editCard, deleteCard, moveCard, reorderCard mutations
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect the state-model module exports.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: All eight mutation functions are exported as callable functions.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: getState returns current board
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call getState() on a fresh state-model instance.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Returns an object with shape { lists: [{ id, name, cards: [{id, title}] }] }.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: subscribe registers change listeners
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Register a subscriber, then call createList('Foo'). Subscriber callback is invoked at least once after the mutation.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Subscriber is called with the new state (or a change notification) within the same tick as the mutation.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: mutations validate inputs and produce predictable state transitions
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call createCard with empty/whitespace title; call moveCard with non-existent listId; call deleteList on missing id.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Invalid inputs are rejected (no state change, throws or returns false); valid inputs produce exactly the expected delta.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: no DOM access anywhere in this module
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Static-scan the state-model source for document, window, HTMLElement references.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: No DOM-related identifiers appear in the module.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: moveCard mutation
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Create two lists L1, L2. Create card C in L1. Call moveCard(C.id, fromListId=L1.id, toListId=L2.id, toIndex=0).
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: C is removed from L1.cards and inserted at L2.cards[0]; getState reflects the move.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: reorderCard mutation
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Create list L with cards [A, B, C]. Call reorderCard(C.id, listId=L.id, toIndex=0).
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: L.cards is now [C, A, B].

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: id generation
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Create 100 lists and 100 cards; collect all ids.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: All ids are unique strings.

out_of_scope: card descriptions or any metadata beyond title (per IP1)

sections.name: persistence
sections.charter: Implement a localStorage adapter that loads board state on startup and saves it after every mutation. Exposes load(), save(state), and clear() functions plus a key constant. Handles JSON parse errors and missing data gracefully by returning null so the app-shell can seed defaults. Pure JS, no DOM. May implement a small debounce around save to avoid thrashing localStorage on rapid mutations, but synchronous save-on-mutation is acceptable.
sections.acceptance: load() returns parsed board state or null if absent/corrupt; save(state) serializes state to localStorage under a stable key; clear() removes the key; corrupt JSON does not throw uncaught; survives page reload.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: load() returns parsed board state or null if absent/corrupt
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Clear localStorage, call load(); then write invalid JSON to the key, call load() again.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Both calls return null without throwing.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: save(state) serializes state to localStorage under a stable key
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call save({lists: [{id:'l1', name:'X', cards:[]}]}); read raw localStorage at the key.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Raw value parses as JSON and deep-equals the input state.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: clear() removes the key
acceptance_assertions.scenario: save(state); clear(); load().
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: load() returns null.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: survives page reload
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Open the app in a browser; create a list named 'My List'; create a card named 'My Card' in it; reload the page (F5 / location.reload).
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: After reload, the board still contains 'My List' with 'My Card' in it, in the same position.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: corrupt JSON does not throw uncaught
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Write the string 'not-json{{{' to the persistence key, then start the app.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: App boots into the seeded default state without an unhandled exception in the console.

sections.name: ui-render
sections.charter: Render the current board state into the DOM: lists as columns containing card elements, plus a 'add list' affordance and an 'add card' affordance per list. Subscribes to the state-model and re-renders on change. Card elements carry data-card-id and data-list-id attributes and have draggable=true so the dnd section can wire onto them. Pure rendering — does not handle drag events or persist; emits no business logic, only DOM.
sections.acceptance: render(rootEl, state) produces one column per list, one card element per card, in the correct order; cards have draggable=true and data attributes for ids; subscription causes re-render on state change; rendering an empty board still shows the 'add list' affordance.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: produces one column per list, one card element per card, in the correct order
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Render state with 2 lists, list A with cards [a1,a2], list B with cards [b1].
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: DOM contains exactly 2 elements matching list selector and 3 matching card selector; document order matches state order.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: cards have draggable=true and data attributes for ids
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Render a state with one card; query the card element.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Element has draggable='true', data-card-id matching the card id, and is inside an element with data-list-id matching its list.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: subscription causes re-render on state change
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Mount renderer; createCard via state-model; observe DOM.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: New card DOM element appears in the correct list without manual re-render call.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: rendering an empty board still shows the 'add list' affordance
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Render state {lists: []}.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: DOM contains the add-list affordance element.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: card title displays on the card
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Open the running app; create a card titled 'Buy milk'.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: The text 'Buy milk' is visibly rendered on the card in the column.

sections.name: dnd
sections.charter: Wire HTML5 drag-and-drop onto the rendered DOM so the user can drag cards between lists and reorder cards within a list. On drop, compute the source list, target list, and target index, then invoke moveCard or reorderCard on the state-model. List headers are not draggable (per IP2). Provides visual feedback during drag (drag-over class on target list) without coupling to specific styling.
sections.acceptance: Dragging a card from list A and dropping it on list B invokes moveCard with the correct ids and a target index; dragging within the same list invokes reorderCard with the correct target index; dropping outside any drop zone is a no-op; drag-over visual hint is applied during a valid hover and removed on drop or cancel.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Dragging a card from list A and dropping it on list B invokes moveCard with the correct ids and a target index
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Programmatically dispatch dragstart on card C (in list A), dragover on list B, drop on list B; spy on state-model.moveCard.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: moveCard is called once with (cardId=C.id, fromListId=A.id, toListId=B.id, toIndex=<computed>).

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: dragging within the same list invokes reorderCard with the correct target index
acceptance_assertions.scenario: List L has cards [X, Y, Z]; drag Z above X; spy on state-model.reorderCard.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: reorderCard is called with (cardId=Z.id, listId=L.id, toIndex=0).

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: dropping outside any drop zone is a no-op
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Dispatch drop on document.body (not inside any list).
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Neither moveCard nor reorderCard is called; state is unchanged.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: drag-over visual hint is applied during a valid hover and removed on drop or cancel
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Dispatch dragenter on list B; check class list. Then dispatch drop or dragend.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: drag-over class (or equivalent) is present during hover and removed afterward.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: user can drag a card and see it appear in the new list
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Open running app with seed lists To Do/Doing/Done and one card 'Task 1' in To Do. Drag 'Task 1' to Doing.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: After release, 'Task 1' is rendered as a child of the Doing column and is no longer in To Do.

out_of_scope: Animations and transitions beyond a basic drag-over class

sections.name: interactions
sections.charter: Wire non-DnD UI events to state-model mutations: clicking 'add list' and submitting a name calls createList; clicking a list title to rename calls renameList; clicking list delete calls deleteList; clicking 'add card' and submitting a title calls createCard; clicking a card to edit calls editCard; clicking card delete calls deleteCard. Uses event delegation on the rendered root so it works after re-renders. Pure event-to-mutation glue; no rendering of its own beyond ephemeral input fields shown via a state flag or inline DOM toggle.
sections.acceptance: Each of the six interactions (add list, rename list, delete list, add card, edit card, delete card) successfully invokes the corresponding state-model mutation; empty/whitespace-only titles are rejected client-side before mutation; deletes are immediate and permanent (per A9).

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: add list interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Click 'add list', type 'Backlog', press Enter.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: A new column titled 'Backlog' appears at the end of the board.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: rename list interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Click on the title of the first list, change to 'Renamed', confirm.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: List title in DOM updates to 'Renamed'.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: delete list interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Click delete control on a list with 0 cards.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: List column is removed from the DOM and from state.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: add card interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: In list 'To Do', click 'add card', type 'Buy milk', confirm.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: A card with title 'Buy milk' appears as the last card in 'To Do'.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: edit card interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Click on an existing card 'Buy milk', change title to 'Buy oat milk', confirm.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Card displays 'Buy oat milk' and state reflects the updated title.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: delete card interaction
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Click delete control on a card.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Card is removed from the DOM and from state.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: empty/whitespace-only titles are rejected client-side before mutation
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Trigger add card with title '' or '   '.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: No createCard mutation is called; no card appears.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: deletes are immediate and permanent
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect UI after delete card; look for undo/restore affordance.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: No undo or trash UI is present (per A9).

out_of_scope: Keyboard shortcuts beyond Enter/Escape on inline inputs

sections.name: integration
sections.charter: Provide index.html and the entry-point app.js that boots the application: load persisted state via persistence; if absent, seed To Do / Doing / Done lists per A11; instantiate the state-model with that initial state; mount ui-render onto a root DOM node; wire dnd and interactions onto the rendered tree; subscribe persistence.save to state-model changes so every mutation is autosaved (per A7). This section is the integrator pseudo-node — it composes the other five sections without adding new business logic.
sections.acceptance: Opening index.html in a modern desktop browser shows a working board; first-load shows To Do / Doing / Done with no cards; subsequent loads restore previous state; every mutation is autosaved without an explicit save button.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Opening index.html in a modern desktop browser shows a working board
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Open index.html in Chrome; observe initial render.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Page renders three columns and an add-list affordance with no console errors.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: first-load shows To Do / Doing / Done with no cards
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Clear localStorage; open index.html.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Three columns titled exactly 'To Do', 'Doing', 'Done' are rendered, in that order, each empty.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: subsequent loads restore previous state
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Add a list 'Custom' and a card 'Persisted'; reload.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: After reload the 'Custom' column with 'Persisted' card is still present alongside the seed columns.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: every mutation is autosaved without an explicit save button
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect UI for any element labeled Save, Save Board, or similar.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: No save control exists; localStorage updates immediately after each mutation.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: autosave-survives-reload
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Create list 'Sprint'; create card 'Refactor'; immediately press F5 (no manual save action).
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: After reload, both 'Sprint' list and 'Refactor' card are still present.

out_of_scope: Build tooling (bundlers, transpilers) — plain ES modules or single bundle

sections.name: edge-case-testing
sections.charter: Cover every acceptance assertion with `verifier: edge_case_testing` from the section list. Every section's acceptance_assertions[] is in scope. Produce executable tests (or test-equivalent harness scripts) that exercise the listed scenarios and assert the expected results, including invalid-input rejection paths, corrupt-storage recovery, drop-outside-zone no-ops, id uniqueness, and re-render correctness after subscriptions fire.
sections.acceptance: All acceptance_assertions from sections 1-6 with verifier=edge_case_testing have a corresponding executable test that passes; tests fail fast and cleanly on regression; coverage report enumerates each assertion id.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: All acceptance_assertions from sections 1-6 with verifier=edge_case_testing have a corresponding executable test
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Cross-reference assertion ids in sections-v1.json with test file contents.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Every edge_case_testing-verified assertion id appears in at least one test name or comment.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: tests fail fast and cleanly on regression
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Intentionally break state-model.createCard to skip validation; run the suite.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: S1.A4 fails with a clear assertion message identifying the broken expectation.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: coverage report enumerates each assertion id
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Run the test harness with reporting enabled.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Output lists each assertion id covered with pass/fail status.

out_of_scope: Visual / pixel-diff testing (cv_artifact_exercise verifier handles UI flows)
